message:
fix(grpc): per-service watch versioning prevents missed status transitions

HealthService watchers previously detected changes by comparing the
current status value against the last-seen status. This meant that
setting a service to Serving, then back to NotServing, then back to
Serving would report no change to a watcher that last saw Serving —
the round-trip was invisible because only the value was compared,
not whether any mutation occurred.

Each service now has its own monotonic version counter in a
watch_versions HashMap, bumped on set_status, clear, and
remove_service when the underlying data actually changes. HealthWatcher
tracks last_version alongside last_status, and changed()/poll_status()
compare versions instead of values. The empty-string service (global
watch) continues to use the existing global version counter.

Tests added: health_watcher_reports_named_service_transient_round_trip
verifies that a Serving→NotServing→Serving round-trip is detected as
two separate changes by both changed() and poll_status() watchers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
